# TC ID Module Scenario Severity Screenshot What This Means (Plain English)
1 TC_NET_001 08 · Network TC_NET_001 Offline banner appears when network disabled High
View
 When WiFi or internet is switched off, the app shows no warning message. Users have no idea why data stopped loading.
2 TC_NET_005 08 · Network TC_NET_005 Load ECG list while offline — graceful error shown High
View
 When trying to view ECG records without internet, the screen gets stuck with no helpful message telling the user what went wrong.
3 TC_PAT_001 03 · Patient Form TC_PAT_001 Valid patient details — risk button enabled High
View
 Even after filling all patient details correctly (name, age, gender, ID), the Get Risk Assessment button stays greyed out and cannot be clicked.
4 TC_PAT_014 03 · Patient Form TC_PAT_014 Negative age → validation error High
View
 If a user types an age below 18 (e.g., age = 1 or 17), the app shows no warning. It should say "Age must be between 18 and 150" but it is silent.
5 TC_RSK_001 04 · Risk Assessment TC_RSK_001 Low risk result → "Low" label visible Critical
View
 After completing all steps for a Low risk ECG, the result is never shown. The app logs the user out mid-process due to session timeout.
6 TC_RSK_002 04 · Risk Assessment TC_RSK_002 Moderate risk result → "Moderate" label visible Critical
View
 After completing all steps for a Moderate risk ECG, the result never appears. The session expires before the result is displayed.
7 TC_RSK_003 04 · Risk Assessment TC_RSK_003 High risk result → "High" label visible Critical
View
 After completing all steps for a High risk ECG, the result never appears. The session expires before the result can be shown to the doctor.
8 TC_OMR_001 11 · Omron Integration TC_OMR_001 Seed low risk ECG → appears and shows Low result High
View
 When a Low risk ECG is sent from the Omron device, it either does not appear in the ECG list or does not show the correct risk result.
9 TC_RSK_010 04 · Risk Assessment (Extended) TC_RSK_010 Feedback question "Was 12 lead ECG done?" visible on result Critical
View
 The question asking the doctor "Was a 12-lead ECG done?" does not appear on the result screen. Clinical feedback cannot be captured.
10 TC_NET_007 08 · Network (Extended) TC_NET_007 Network restored — ECG list can be refreshed High
View
 After the internet comes back, the ECG list does not automatically refresh. Users may think their records are gone.
11 TC_NET_009 08 · Network (Extended) TC_NET_009 Very slow 2G network — app still renders High No screenshot On a very slow connection (like 2G or rural network), the app completely fails to load — leaving the user on a blank screen.
12 TC_SRC_004 06 · Search TC_SRC_004 Clear search restores full list Medium
View
 After searching for a patient and then clearing the search bar (pressing X), the full list of ECGs does not come back. The screen stays empty.
13 TC_SRC_010 06 · Search (Extended) TC_SRC_010 Empty search input shows full ECG list Medium
View
 When the search bar is left completely empty, all ECG records should be shown. Instead, the list stays empty or incomplete.
14 TC_PAT_BB_003 15 · Patient Form Black Box (Positive) TC_PAT_BB_003 Age = 99 (midpoint) — accepted High
View
 A perfectly valid age of 99 years is being rejected by the form — the risk assessment button does not activate, blocking the entire workflow.
15 TC_ECG_BB_006 16 · ECG Flow Black Box (Positive) TC_ECG_BB_006 Risk result page shows patient name entered in form Medium
View
 The patient name that was typed into the Patient Information Form is missing from the final risk result screen.
16 TC_ECG_BB_007 16 · ECG Flow Black Box (Positive) TC_ECG_BB_007 Risk result page shows correct age entered Medium
View
 The patient age that was typed into the Patient Information Form is missing from the final risk result screen.
17 TC_ECG_BB_009 16 · ECG Flow Black Box (Positive) TC_ECG_BB_009 ECG detail shows ECG waveform strip Medium
View
 The ECG heart trace (waveform image) is not appearing on the ECG detail page. Doctors cannot visually review the trace.
18 TC_LGN_BB_003 14 · Auth Black Box (Positive) TC_LGN_BB_003 Enter key submits login form Medium
View
 Pressing the Enter key after typing a password does not log the user in. The doctor must manually click the Login button — poor usability.
19 TC_LGN_BB_005 14 · Auth Black Box (Positive) TC_LGN_BB_005 Correct credentials after wrong attempt — login succeeds Medium
View
 If a user types the wrong password first and then types the correct one, the app still refuses to log them in. They must refresh the page.
20 TC_LGN_BB_018 14 · Auth Black Box (Edge) TC_LGN_BB_018 Very long email (300 chars) — graceful handling, no crash Medium
View
 Pasting a very long email address (300+ characters) causes JavaScript errors in the app — a crash risk that should be handled gracefully.
21 TC_LGN_BB_019 14 · Auth Black Box (Edge) TC_LGN_BB_019 Very long password (300 chars) — graceful handling Medium
View
 Pasting a very long password (300+ characters) causes JavaScript errors. The app should quietly reject it without crashing.
22 TC_LGN_BB_020 14 · Auth Black Box (Edge) TC_LGN_BB_020 5 rapid failed logins — no crash, still functional Medium No screenshot After 5 failed login attempts in quick succession, the login form becomes completely unresponsive. Users are unable to type in their credentials.
23 TC_UX_BB_005 17 · UX & Accessibility Black Box (Positive) TC_UX_BB_005 App version is visible somewhere in the UI High
View
 The app version number is not displayed anywhere on the login screen, making it impossible to verify which version is currently installed.
24 TC_UX_BB_010 17 · UX & Accessibility Black Box (Positive) TC_UX_BB_010 App renders at 150% browser zoom without broken layout High
View
 When the browser zoom is increased to 150% (common for users with vision difficulties), the app layout breaks and key elements disappear.
25 TC_UX_BB_017 17 · UX & Accessibility Black Box (Edge) TC_UX_BB_017 Forgot password link visible on login page High
View
 The "Forgot Password" link is not visible on the login page. Users who forget their password have no way to reset it from the login screen.
⚠️ Critical App Bug: Session Expiry Mid-Workflow (Affects 6 Tests)
Root Cause: The CardioCheck app session expires in approximately 3–5 minutes. Long test workflows (ECG seed → patient form fill → risk assessment API call) regularly exceed this window, causing the app to silently redirect to /login mid-flow. When the session is lost before the risk result is rendered, the export icon never appears and these tests skip their precondition guard (if exportBtn.count === 0 → test.skip).

Affected Tests: TC_RSK_002 (Moderate risk — Fail), TC_RPT_002, TC_RPT_003, TC_RPT_008, TC_RPT_009, TC_RPT_011 (Skipped)

Impact: Real-world users performing the same flow (open ECG → fill patient data → await risk result) would be silently logged out mid-session, losing their work with no warning — a Critical UX and data safety issue.

Recommended Fix: Increase session TTL to ≥ 30 minutes, implement silent token refresh on active use, and add an explicit session-expiry warning dialog before auto-logout.

Test Coverage Highlights
  • ✅ Authentication — 23 scenarios incl. SQL injection, XSS, brute force, session, EULA
  • ✅ Security — 16 scenarios: auth bypass, injection, HTTPS, token exposure, route guards
  • ✅ HIPAA — 10 scenarios: PHI in URLs/storage, data minimization, auth control, back-nav
  • ✅ Patient Form — 28 scenarios: all boundary values (age 18/150), validation, special chars
  • ✅ Risk Assessment — 14 scenarios: Low/Moderate/High results, feedback, export, waveform
  • ✅ Performance — 7 benchmarks: login <10s, list <5s, risk <60s, PDF <10s
  • ✅ UX / End-User — 12 scenarios: labels, error language, date format, confirmation dialogs
  • ✅ Network — 9 scenarios: offline, restore, 2G, mid-flow disconnect
